name: help
description: LambdaMOO-shaped in-world help database classes and a seeded global help database.

classes.description: Generic help database. It stores static topic values in a topics map, resolves exact and abbreviated topic names, renders compact topic output, and records missed lookups for later documentation work.

verbs.name: find_topics
verbs.source: verb :find_topics(topic) rxd { return null; /* native: see help_db_find_topics */ }

verbs.name: get_topic
verbs.source: verb :get_topic(topic, remaining_dbs) rxd { return null; /* native: see help_db_get_topic */ }

verbs.name: dump_topic
verbs.source: verb :dump_topic(topic) rxd { return null; /* native: see help_db_dump_topic */ }

verbs.name: record_miss
verbs.source: verb :record_miss(topic) rxd { return null; /* native: see help_db_record_miss */ }

verbs.name: look_self
verbs.source: verb :look_self() rxd { return { title: this.name, description: this.description, topics: this:find_topics("") }; }

seed_hooks.name: Help
seed_hooks.description: Global help database for first-light worlds. Catalogs and classes can add their own help databases later; this one supplies the baseline commands and conventions.

help: Type help <topic> for a topic, or help index for the topic list.
Topic names accept abbreviations when they are unambiguous. Leading @ is ignored, so help @help and help help resolve the same way.
commands: Common commands include look, look <thing>, say <text>, emote <text>, tell <person> <text>, go <exit>, take <thing>, and drop <thing>.
The exact verbs available come from the object you are in, the objects near you, and your player class.
movement: Rooms expose exits as verbs such as north, west, southeast, out, or go <exit>.
Moving through an exit calls the exit object, which moves you and announces the departure and arrival through the source and destination rooms.
chat: Speech is live and direct. say sends text to the current room; emote, pose, quote, and tell are conversational variants.
look asks the target to describe itself with look_self; room output is composed by the room.
building: Builder and programmer tools live on player classes. Builders create and arrange objects; programmers install and edit source.
Use the programmer MCP tools or enter the verb editor when you have the appropriate class and progbit.
